g.text: Δ → Larger n**\nDetecting small effects requires more samples\n
Power → Larger n**\n80% power is standard minimum\n
Variance → Larger n**\nNoisy data needs more samples\n

Symbol | Definition | Typical Value |
|--------|------------|---------------|
| α | Significance level | 0.05 |
| β | Type II error rate | 0.20 |
| 1-β | Statistical power | 0.80 |
| σ² | Population variance | Data-dependent |
| Δ | Minimum detectable effect | Business-defined |
| n | Sample size per group | Calculated |

α = 0.05 → Z_α = 1.96
- β = 0.20 → Z_β = 0.84
- σ² = 0.25 (conversion rate variance)
- Δ = 0.05 (5% minimum detectable effect)

**Calculation:**

n = 2 × (1.96 + 0.84)² × 0.25 / 0.05²
n = 2 × 7.84 × 0.25 / 0.0025
n = 1,568 per group

**Total sample needed: 3,136 users**
